genes.gene_name: SUS1; SUS2; SUS3; SUS4; SUS5; SUS6

Source_Species.reason: The paper studies Arabidopsis mutants and explicitly states Arabidopsis thaliana was used.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Explicitly stated in Plant material and growth conditions: Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The wild-type control is explicitly given as ecotype Columbia-0 (Col-0).

Applied_Species.reason: All experiments were conducted in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The study uses Col-0 as the wild-type background/control for the sus mutants.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Cellulose content and UDP-glucose content were both directly measured in Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Cellulose is a polysaccharide and UDP-glucose is a carbohydrate nucleotide; this classification is directly consistent with the metabolites studied.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Cellulose was quantified in stems and rosette leaves, which corresponds to cell wall cellulose; UDP-glucose was explicitly quantified in rosette leaves.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The Summary states UDP-glucose is the substrate for cellulose biosynthesis and an intermediate for other matrix polysaccharides; cellulose as a structural wall polysaccharide is directly implied throughout.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results explicitly report no visible growth differences, no vascular/anatomical defects, no cellulose changes, and increased UDP-glucose in rosette leaves of sus mutants.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each point matches the Results: no growth defects, no collapsed xylem/hypocotyl/cellulose phenotype, and UDP-glucose increased by 10.6%, 21.0%, and 22.4% in susquad, sussext-1, and sussext-2.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The paper states normal growth under long-day or short-day conditions and a dark-grown hypocotyl assay.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No subcellular localization for SUS proteins is stated in this paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper explicitly refers to sucrose synthase activity/enzyme and reversible sucrose cleavage, but does not state a protein family/domain designation such as glycosyltransferase family.

Omics_Data.reason: The study directly performed targeted LC-MS/MS quantification of UDP-glucose, and no accession number is reported.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper reports no growth penalty under tested conditions and discusses prior reports of growth defects under hypoxia in sus1sus4 mutants, indicating stress-conditional importance.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are explicitly described and used in Results: mutant genetics, cellulose assay, anatomy microscopy, hypocotyl assay, and LC-MS/MS UDP-glucose quantification.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an interpretation/application extrapolation not explicitly discussed in the paper, though it is broadly consistent with the findings.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed treats the SUS family collectively via quadruple and sextuple mutants and concludes SUS activity is dispensable for cellulose biosynthesis under tested conditions due to alternative UDP-glucose pathway(s).

corrections.reason: No subcellular localization for SUS proteins is stated in this paper.

Source_Species.reason: The pgm mutant is explicitly described as an Arabidopsis mutant.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The species throughout the study is Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The paper explicitly states the starchless pgm mutant germplasm CS3092 was obtained from ABRC.

Applied_Species.reason: The control experiment was performed in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The study organism is Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The paper identifies the pgm line as germplasm CS3092.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: UDP-glucose was directly measured in rosette leaf extracts of pgm.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: UDP-glucose is a carbohydrate nucleotide; this is a correct classification of the measured metabolite.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results explicitly state UDP-glucose was analyzed in rosette leaf extracts.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states UDP-glucose is the substrate for cellulose biosynthesis and intermediate for other matrix polysaccharides, supporting its central donor role.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results explicitly say pgm exhibits reduced UDP-glucose levels compared with wild type at the end of the dark period and was used as an additional control.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements are directly supported: pgm was used as a control, had reduced UDP-glucose in line with prior results, and corroborated the LC-MS/MS analysis.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The paper explicitly says pgm was analyzed at the end of the night in short-day-grown plants.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The paper does not state the subcellular localization of PGM.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper does not identify PGM as phosphoglucomutase or give a protein family/domain description; it only names the pgm mutant.

Omics_Data.reason: UDP-glucose was quantified by targeted LC-MS/MS and no accession number is reported.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The pgm result comes from mutant comparison and targeted LC-MS/MS metabolite quantification.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This value is a secondary interpretation; the paper does not discuss application value for pgm.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper clearly includes pgm as an additional control in Results rather than as the primary target of the cellulose/SUS study.

corrections.reason: The paper does not state the subcellular localization of PGM.
The paper does not identify PGM as phosphoglucomutase or give a protein family/domain description; it only names the pgm mutant.
